questions.stem: What is being done in the U.S. to stop damage to the stratospheric ozone layer?
options: Using coal instead of nuclear power to produce electricity
Eliminating the use of CFCs and HCFCs refrigerants
Enforcing strict emission requirements on two-cycle engines
All of the above

questions.stem: How much can a technician be fined for venting a non-exempt refrigerant from a system?
options: $14,439 per day per violation
$27,539 per day per violation
$37,839 per day per violation
$44,539 per day per violation

questions.stem: What is a violation of the Clean Air Act or EPA's regulations?
options: Failing to keep required records
Failing to meet the required evacuation levels before opening or disposing of appliances
Knowingly releasing CFC, HCFC, or HFC refrigerants while repairing appliances
All of the above

questions.stem: State and local governments __________________.
options: Cannot make any laws related to refrigerant recovery
Can establish laws to reduce the federal refrigerant recovery requirements
Can make laws that are stricter requirements than the federal regulations
Can temporarily suspend the prohibition on the manufacture of R-22 for up to 6 months

questions.stem: The ___________ program identifies alternative refrigerants for use.
options: SNAP
E-Star
The ASHRAE-approved (AA)
Energy Star

questions.stem: The Montreal Protocol is ________________________.
options: An international treaty that addresses ozone-depleting substances and their alternatives
A test procedure or protocol to use when measuring GWP
A test procedure or protocol to use when measuring ODP
An international treaty that certified technicians and recovery equipment

questions.stem: The production and import of new R-22 in the U.S. to service existing systems is banned beginning in ________.
After a refrigerant is phased-out, supplies of that refrigerant for equipment servicing can only come from __________________.
options: Recovered and reclaimed gas
Third-World countries
Chinese and Indian manufacturers under a special permit
Exemptions granted under the Montreal Protocol

questions.stem: Technicians who violate Clean Air Act provisions __________________.
options: Can lose their certification
Will have their equipment confiscated
Will be required to take mandatory training
May be required to pass an online test

questions.stem: Which is a violation of EPA or Clean Air Act regulations?
options: Failing to evacuate refrigerant in a system to the required evacuation level
Failing to evacuate refrigerant in a leaking system to at least 0 psig
Adding nitrogen to a fully charged system, and then calling the mixture a 'leak check gas' and venting the mixture to the atmosphere
All of the above

questions.stem: Reclaimed refrigerant must be tested to meet __________________ before it can be resold.
options: AHRI 700
AEPA 700
ASHRAE 700
UL 700

questions.stem: If technicians lose their certification card, they must_________________.
options: Order a replacement from the EPA within 100 days
Request a replacement from the company that provided the certification
Have the refrigerant supply house certify to the EPA that they had a certification card
Order a replacement from the EPA within 30 days or face permanent loss of certification

questions.stem: Distributors must verify __________________ before they can sell refrigerant.
options: The quality of the refrigerant
That the net weight on the refrigerant container is accurate
That the purchaser owns a certified recovery device
That the purchaser employs at least one Section 608 certified technician

questions.stem: What does NOT require Section 608 certification?
options: Recovering refrigerant
Rewiring a condenser fan
Replacing an evaporator
Attaching a piercing self-tapping Schrader valve

questions.stem: What refrigerant servicing does NOT require Section 608 certification?
options: Carbon dioxide refrigerant
Propane

questions.stem: Which of the following is a lawful use of HCFC-22?
options: Assembling 'dry-charge' components into a system and then charging with reclaimed R-22
Servicing existing R-22 systems
Charging any new system designed for R-407C with reclaimed or recovered R-22
All of the above

questions.stem: When servicing an R-22 system you CANNOT _______.
options: Add R-22 to top off the system after the repair is completed
Retrofit the unit to R-407C
Use recovered or recycled R-22 from the same owner
Top off the unit with R-407C

questions.stem: How should disposable cylinders be discarded?
options: Vent the remaining refrigerant and render the cylinder useless.
Recover all remaining refrigerant, render the cylinders useless, and recycle the metal.
Don't discard them; approved recyclers can refill them for reuse.
Reuse the cylinders as nitrogen gas tanks.

questions.stem: Before disposing of any appliance containing refrigerant __________________.
options: Recover the refrigerant
Vent the refrigerant so safety issues are not violated and no one is harmed
Purge the refrigerant with nitrogen
Seal the appliance to prevent any refrigerant from escaping

questions.stem: The EPA's 'safe disposal requirements' make the __________________ responsible for ensuring that all refrigerant has been removed from household refrigerators before they are disposed.
options: Final person in the disposal chain
Equipment owner
Last person to service the unit
Either B or C

questions.stem: All CFC, HCFC, and HFC refrigerant recovery devices must _________________.
options: Be portable
Contain a heavy-duty splash shield
Have a two-stage vacuum pump
Meet EPA standards

questions.stem: Which appliances are NOT covered by EPA regulations?
options: Mini-fridges
Ice makers
Commercial refrigeration equipment
Desiccant dehumidifiers

questions.stem: A 'self-contained' recovery device ________________.
options: Cannot be used anymore
Removes refrigerant with the assistance of components in the appliance
Must have the recovery tank inside the recovery unit
Can only be used on a system with 5 pounds or less of refrigerant

questions.stem: EPA regulations require appliances to have ___________.
options: A service aperture or process stub
Purge valves or purge units
King valves
Oversized filter driers

questions.stem: A ________________ recovery device uses the compressor in the appliance and/or the pressure of the refrigerant in the appliance.
options: System-dependent
Pressure-dependent
Compressor-dependent
Self-sufficient

questions.stem: When disposing of an appliance with 5-50 pounds of refrigerant, you must record_______________.
options: The type of refrigerant recovered
The quantity of refrigerant, by type, recovered from the appliance during the past 12 months
The quantity of refrigerant, by type, sent for reclamation or destruction
All of the above

questions.stem: What happens when EPA regulations change after a technician is certified?
options: The technician has 18 months to get recertified.
The technician has a responsibility to keep up with changes in EPA regulations.
If certified after 2002, the technician has 18 months to get recertified.
The technician must attend an 8-hour refresher class to become recertified.

questions.stem: What does a Section 608 certification allow a technician to do?
options: Import virgin CFC from other countries
Purchase or handle controlled refrigerants
Service motor vehicle air conditioners using R-134a only
Service any type of motor vehicle air conditioner in electric vehicles

questions.stem: What action violates the EPA's prohibition on venting?
options: Small accidental release of refrigerant when servicing a unit
Opening the system without properly recovering the refrigerant first
A catastrophic failure of a system
Refrigerant lost when connecting or disconnecting low loss hoses

questions.stem: All refrigerants _________________.
options: Deplete the stratospheric ozone layer
Contain chlorine
Must be recovered before opening or disposing of appliances
Are toxic to sea life
